headline: Plastic Pollution Costs Malaysia RM6.5B Annually
context: A 2025 World Bank study estimated Malaysia's annual economic cost from plastic pollution at RM6.5 billion, including marine damage, tourism loss, and healthcare impacts.

cards.big: Malaysia generates 38,000 tonnes of plastic waste daily.
cards.sub: Only 24% is recycled. The rest enters landfills, rivers, or the ocean.

cards.big: Malaysia became the world's top plastic waste importer in 2018.
cards.sub: After China's ban, 150 illegal recycling factories opened in Jenjarom alone. 40 remain operational.

cards.big: The Roadmap Towards Zero Single-Use Plastics 2018 set a 2030 deadline.
cards.sub: Midpoint review shows only 2 of 9 milestones are on track.

cards.big: Extended Producer Responsibility begins in 2026 covering 6 product categories.
cards.sub: Industry compliance costs are estimated at RM1.2B — less than 20% of annual pollution costs.

cards.big: The country that imports waste to recycle it cannot manage the waste it creates.
EPR is a start, but without enforcement infrastructure and informal sector integration, it will replicate the pattern of ambitious targets and weak follow-through.
